alteryx.name: Alteryx
alteryx.short_description: Alteryx is a self-service data analytics platform for drag-and-drop data prep, blending, and automated analytics.
alteryx.starting_price: List price around $5,195/user/year for Alteryx Designer; enterprise deployments are quoted individually

features.description: Build data preparation and analytics pipelines by connecting prebuilt tool icons on a canvas instead of writing code.
features.title: Visual drag-and-drop workflow canvas

features.description: Connect to spreadsheets, databases, cloud warehouses, and business applications, then join and reconcile them into a single dataset.
features.title: Data blending from multiple sources

features.description: Built-in tools for regression, clustering, and other statistical models without needing a separate stats package.
features.title: Predictive and statistical analytics tools

features.description: Geocoding, drive-time analysis, and location-based joins built directly into the same workflow canvas.
features.title: Spatial and location analytics

features.description: Schedule workflows to run automatically and share, govern, and audit them across an organization.
features.title: Alteryx Server automation and scheduling

features.description: A searchable catalog of data assets and existing workflows so teams can discover and reuse prior work.
features.title: Alteryx Connect data catalog

features.description: Cloud-based access to workflow building, automation, and reporting for teams that want a browser-based experience.
features.title: Alteryx Analytics Cloud

features.description: Auto Insights and related AI features automatically surface patterns, anomalies, and drivers within a dataset.
features.title: AI-assisted insight generation

features.description: A library of reusable building blocks and a marketplace of community and vendor add-ons to speed up workflow creation.
features.title: Prebuilt connectors, macros, and templates

features.description: Tools to generate formatted reports, charts, and outputs directly from a workflow, in addition to feeding downstream BI tools.
features.title: Reporting and output tools

pros: No-code visual workflow builder lowers the barrier to advanced data prep for non-programmers
Strong data blending capabilities across disparate sources like spreadsheets, databases, and cloud apps
Predictive and spatial analytics are built into the same tool used for everyday data prep
Large library of free training (Alteryx Academy) and an active community with recurring practice challenges
Server and governance features scale workflows from a single analyst up to enterprise-wide automation
cons: Pricing is quote-based and opaque, and list pricing is high relative to spreadsheet or BI-only tools
No free tier for ongoing production use, only trials, which can be a barrier for very small teams
Learning curve for advanced modules (predictive, spatial, automation) is steeper than basic data prep
Historically Windows-desktop-centric for the core Designer product, though cloud options are expanding
Complex, resource-heavy workflows can require significant local or server compute to run efficiently

faqs.answer: Alteryx is used to prepare, blend, and analyze data from multiple sources through a visual, drag-and-drop workflow instead of manual spreadsheet work or custom code, and to automate those workflows on a schedule.
faqs.question: What is Alteryx used for?

faqs.answer: No. Alteryx is designed for business and finance analysts to build data workflows visually, though it also supports embedding SQL, Python, or R code for users who want more advanced control.
faqs.question: Does Alteryx require coding skills?

faqs.answer: Alteryx does not offer a permanent free tier for production use, but it does offer a free trial so prospective customers can evaluate the platform before purchasing a license.
faqs.question: Is there a free version or free trial of Alteryx?

faqs.answer: Designer is the desktop application analysts use to build individual workflows, while Server is used to schedule, share, and govern those workflows across a team or organization.
faqs.question: What is the difference between Alteryx Designer and Alteryx Server?

faqs.answer: Alteryx is priced per user on an annual subscription and sold largely through custom, quote-based enterprise contracts; published list pricing for the core Designer product has historically been around $5,195 per user per year, with the final price depending on volume and modules.
faqs.question: How much does Alteryx cost?

features: Scheduling and automation of shared workflows
Integration with Alteryx Connect data catalog
Access to prebuilt connectors and templates

microsoft-power-bi.name: Microsoft Power BI
microsoft-power-bi.short_description: Microsoft's BI and data visualization platform for building interactive reports and dashboards from virtually any data source.

features.description: Connect to and transform data from hundreds of sources, including databases, cloud services, and spreadsheets, before it enters your model.
features.title: Power Query

features.description: Build relationships between tables and write custom calculations and measures using DAX to create a reusable semantic model.
features.title: DAX & Data Modeling

features.description: Drag-and-drop report design with drill-through, bookmarks, and filtering for exploring data visually.
features.title: Interactive Reports & Dashboards

features.description: Ask questions about your data in plain English and get an automatically generated chart or answer.
features.title: Natural Language Q&A

features.description: Built-in forecasting, clustering, and key influencer visuals, plus Copilot-assisted report creation and narrative summaries.
features.title: AI Visuals & Copilot

features.description: Control what each user can see within a shared dataset, and certify or endorse trusted reports across the organization.
features.title: Row-Level Security & Governance

features.description: Keep cloud reports current with scheduled and incremental refresh, including secure connections to on-premises data via gateways.
features.title: Scheduled Refresh & Gateways

features.description: Create pixel-perfect, printable operational reports for invoices, statements, and other fixed-layout documents.
features.title: Paginated Reports

features.description: View and interact with dashboards on iOS, Android, and Windows devices while away from a desktop.
features.title: Mobile Apps

features.description: Embed reports in Teams, SharePoint, and Power Apps, and trigger workflows with Power Automate.
features.title: Power Platform Integration

pros: Free Power BI Desktop is fully featured for building and modeling reports, so individuals can start without paying upfront
Deep integration with Excel, Teams, SharePoint, and Azure lowers friction for organizations already on Microsoft 365
DAX and Power Query give advanced users precise control over calculations and data transformation beyond drag-and-drop charting
Enterprise-grade governance, including row-level security, certified datasets, and deployment pipelines, supports large, IT-managed analytics programs
Frequent monthly feature updates from Microsoft keep the platform evolving quickly, including new AI-assisted capabilities
cons: Licensing can get complex once you factor in Pro, Premium Per User, and capacity-based Premium tiers
DAX and proper data modeling have a real learning curve for users expecting simple spreadsheet-style charting
Power BI Desktop is Windows-only, so Mac users need a browser or a virtual machine to author reports
Sharing and collaboration features require paid licensing, such as Pro or Premium, even for small teams
Large or complex data models can hit performance limits without capacity-level Premium licensing or careful optimization

faqs.answer: Power BI Desktop, the report-authoring app, is free to download and use. Sharing reports with others, scheduling refreshes, and collaborating in the Power BI service generally require a paid Pro or Premium license per user.
faqs.question: Is Power BI free?

faqs.answer: Pro is a per-user license enabling sharing and collaboration. Premium Per User adds larger dataset limits, paginated reports, and more advanced AI features per user. Premium capacity is an organization-wide license providing dedicated compute for larger workloads, priced per capacity rather than per seat.
faqs.question: What's the difference between Power BI Pro and Premium?

faqs.answer: Power BI Desktop is a Windows-only application. Mac users typically access reports through the Power BI service in a web browser or run Desktop via a Windows virtual machine.
faqs.question: Does Power BI work on Mac?

faqs.answer: Yes, Power BI includes connectors for hundreds of data sources, including SQL Server, Excel, SharePoint, Salesforce, Google Analytics, and various Azure services, plus a data gateway for secure on-premises refresh.
faqs.question: Can Power BI connect to my existing databases and cloud apps?

faqs.answer: Basic report building and drag-and-drop visualizations don't require code, but getting the most out of Power BI, including custom calculations and advanced data modeling, benefits from learning DAX and the Power Query M language.
faqs.question: Do I need to know how to code to use Power BI?

features: Full report authoring and data modeling

servicenow.name: ServiceNow
servicenow.short_description: Enterprise cloud platform that automates IT service management, IT operations, HR, and customer service workflows with AI.

features.description: Manages incidents, problems, changes, and requests with structured workflows, SLAs, and a configurable service catalog.
features.title: IT Service Management (ITSM)

features.description: Discovers infrastructure, maps service dependencies, and correlates events to speed up root-cause identification.
features.title: IT Operations Management (ITOM)

features.description: Uses generative AI embedded in workflows to summarize cases, draft knowledge content, and recommend next steps.
features.title: Now Assist (Generative AI)

features.description: A low-code environment for building custom applications and forms that reuse existing platform data and workflow logic.
features.title: App Engine

features.description: Digitizes employee case management, onboarding, and HR requests into trackable, auditable workflows.
features.title: HR Service Delivery

features.description: Provides case management and omnichannel support tools for handling external customer inquiries at scale.
features.title: Customer Service Management (CSM)

features.description: A conversational chatbot that resolves common requests and routes complex ones to the right human agent.
features.title: Virtual Agent

features.description: Connects ServiceNow to other enterprise systems through prebuilt connectors, APIs, and a visual flow designer.
features.title: Integration Hub

features.description: Links vulnerability response and security incident response into the same workflow and prioritization engine.
features.title: Security Operations

features.description: Delivers dashboards and reporting to track KPIs and workflow performance across departments.
features.title: Performance Analytics

pros: Highly configurable workflow platform that spans IT, HR, customer service, and custom applications
Enterprise-grade scalability, governance, and security suited to large, complex organizations
Extensive integration ecosystem and marketplace of prebuilt connectors and applications
Growing generative AI capabilities embedded directly into everyday service workflows
Mature partner network and certification programs to support large implementations
cons: No published pricing; every deployment requires a custom sales quote
Steep learning curve that typically requires dedicated administrators or implementation partners
Can be cost-prohibitive for small businesses or teams with narrow, single-purpose needs
Implementation projects can be lengthy depending on scope and number of workflows configured
Licensing that scales with users or agents can become expensive as adoption grows

faqs.answer: ServiceNow does not publish standard pricing. Costs depend on the products, modules, and number of users or agents licensed, and are quoted through the sales team on a custom contract basis.
faqs.question: How much does ServiceNow cost?

faqs.answer: ServiceNow is mainly used to automate and track workflows such as IT incident and change management, employee HR requests, customer service cases, and custom business processes on a single platform.
faqs.question: What is ServiceNow mainly used for?

faqs.answer: No. While it originated in IT service management, ServiceNow is now used broadly across HR, customer service, security operations, and other departments that need structured, trackable workflows.
faqs.question: Is ServiceNow only for IT departments?

faqs.answer: ServiceNow does not offer a standard self-serve free trial; prospective customers typically request a demo or proof of concept through the sales team.
faqs.question: Does ServiceNow offer a free trial?

faqs.answer: Now Assist is ServiceNow's generative AI capability, embedded across its workflow products to summarize records, draft responses, and suggest next steps for agents and employees.
faqs.question: What is Now Assist?

features: Core incident, problem, and change management
Broader access to security, HR, and customer service modules

cons: Pricing is not published and requires a sales-led, enterprise procurement process

faqs.answer: Planview is used by enterprises to manage strategic portfolios, projects, products, and resources in one connected system, giving leadership visibility into how work aligns with business strategy.
faqs.question: What is Planview used for?

faqs.answer: Planview does not publish standard pricing. Costs depend on which products, modules, and number of users an organization needs, and are quoted through a sales conversation.
faqs.question: How much does Planview cost?

faqs.answer: Yes. Planview includes Lean-Agile delivery boards and portfolio tools designed to support Kanban and scaled agile frameworks alongside traditional project management.
faqs.question: Does Planview support agile teams?

faqs.answer: Generally no. Planview is built for large and midsize enterprises with complex portfolios; small teams needing simple task tracking are usually better served by lighter tools.
faqs.question: Is Planview suitable for small businesses?

faqs.answer: Yes, Planview has introduced AI-assisted capabilities under the Planview Copilot branding that help surface portfolio insights and reduce manual reporting effort.
faqs.question: Does Planview offer AI features?

features.description: Supports S/MIME and PGP encryption, GDPR compliance, and SAML SSO.
features.title: Security features

pros: Core software is completely free and open source under AGPL-3.0
Unlimited agents, tickets, and mailboxes with no per-seat pricing
Native mobile apps for Android and iOS
Broad channel support including SMS, WhatsApp, and Telegram
cons: Requires self-hosting on PHP/MySQL infrastructure, needing technical setup
Advanced modules (Kanban, live chat, CRM, SMS) may require paid add-ons
Cloud-hosted options are only available via third-party providers, not officially from FreeScout

faqs.answer: Yes, the core FreeScout software is free and open source under the AGPL-3.0 license.
faqs.question: Is FreeScout really free?

faqs.answer: Yes, FreeScout is designed to be self-hosted on PHP/MySQL infrastructure, though third-party providers offer cloud hosting.
faqs.question: Do I need to self-host FreeScout?

faqs.answer: It's built with PHP using the Laravel framework.
faqs.question: What is FreeScout built with?

faqs.answer: Yes, it offers dedicated Android and iOS apps in addition to a mobile-friendly web interface.
faqs.question: Does FreeScout have mobile apps?

boundary.name: Boundary
boundary.short_description: Boundary is HashiCorp's identity-based access management tool for securely accessing infrastructure without exposing credentials or networks.

features.description: Enable privileged sessions for users and applications based on identity and role.
features.title: Identity-based access

features.description: Automates onboarding of infrastructure resources as hosts.
features.title: Automated service discovery

features.description: Define access policies and configuration as code with the full Terraform provider.
features.title: Terraform provider support

features.description: Visibility into session metrics, events, logs, and traces, with export to monitoring tools.
features.title: Session auditing

features.description: Injects SSH credentials for sessions instead of exposing them to end users.
features.title: SSH credential injection

pros: Identity-based access removes the need for VPNs or shared credentials
Integrates with Terraform for infrastructure-as-code policy management
Session auditing with export to monitoring tools
Both self-managed Community and managed HCP Boundary options available
cons: HCP Boundary pricing beyond the free trial is not published on the site
Requires integration work with existing identity providers
Fewer built-in integrations than more established PAM tools

faqs.answer: A self-managed Community edition is available; HCP Boundary (managed) offers a free trial.
faqs.question: Is Boundary free?

faqs.answer: Secure, identity-based remote access to infrastructure without exposing networks or sharing credentials.
faqs.question: What problem does Boundary solve?

faqs.answer: Yes, it has full Terraform provider support for policies and configuration.
faqs.question: Does Boundary integrate with Terraform?

faqs.answer: HashiCorp's managed cloud offering for Boundary, available with a free trial.
faqs.question: What is HCP Boundary?

redash.name: Redash
redash.short_description: Redash is an open source tool for querying multiple data sources and building shareable SQL dashboards, now maintained under Databricks.

features.description: A query interface with schema browsing and click-to-insert query building.
features.title: SQL editor

features.description: Build and customize dashboards from query results.
features.title: Drag-and-drop dashboards

features.description: Connects to SQL, NoSQL, Big Data, and API-based data sources.
features.title: Broad data source support

features.description: Automatically refreshes data from connected sources on a schedule.
features.title: Scheduled refresh

features.description: Share dashboards with teammates or the public, and set alerts on query results.
features.title: Sharing and alerting

features.description: Save query snippets for reuse across dashboards.
features.title: Reusable query snippets

pros: Fully open source with no vendor lock-in and self-hosting support
Connects to a wide range of SQL, NoSQL, and API data sources
Simple, lightweight setup for teams that just need SQL dashboards
cons: Owned by Databricks since 2020 and now in maintenance mode, with infrequent releases
The original hosted Redash Cloud service was shut down in November 2021
The community forum is read-only, limiting official support channels
Lacks the AI and semantic-layer features found in newer open source BI tools

faqs.answer: Yes, Redash is an open source, self-hosted project with no licensing cost.
faqs.question: Is Redash free?

faqs.answer: Redash has been in maintenance mode since Databricks acquired it in 2020, with infrequent releases.
faqs.question: Is Redash still actively developed?

faqs.answer: No, the original Redash Cloud hosted service was discontinued in November 2021; only self-hosting is currently available.
faqs.question: Is there a hosted version of Redash?

faqs.answer: It supports SQL, NoSQL, Big Data, and API-based data sources.
faqs.question: What data sources does Redash support?
